items.title: Yaya Toure agrees shock next job in management and set to bring in former EFL midfielder as his assistant
items.summary: MANCHESTER CITY icon Yaya Toure has agreed to become the new head coach of Slovan Bratislava. The legendary midfielder has left his post as Saudi Arabia assistant manager under Georgios Donis. Speaking of number twos, Toure has recruited ex-Celtic and Swansea City coach Darren O’Dea as his assistant

items.title: Nick Pope offered Newcastle transfer escape route by two Premier League rivals as Eddie Howe closes in on first signing
items.summary: LEEDS UNITED and Ipswich are battling it out to sign Newcastle’s Nick Pope. The England international goalkeeper, 34, is now weighing up whether to end his four-year stay on Tyneside or fight for his place. items.summary: FABIO CAPELLO is tipping Norway and Erling Haaland to become the surprise package of this summer’s World Cup. items.title: England veterans WIN Seniors World Cup as team filled with ex-Premier League stars hammer France
items.summary: IT is coming home for England Seniors who battered France 4-0 to lift the 18th Seniors World Cup in Thailand. items.title: Paraguay star Enciso injured in World Cup warm-up
items.summary: Paraguay forward Julio Enciso's World Cup is in doubt after he was injured during a 4-0 friendly win over Nicaragua.

items.title: Who is Real Madrid’s mystery £130m transfer target? President Perez’s amazing pledge sparks chaos ahead of election
items.summary: REAL MADRID president Florentino Perez sparked chaos by announcing that he will bid €150m (£130m) for a global superstar on Tuesday, should he win the club's election.
